items.name: Defining the Architecture
items.description: Decentralized Finance refers to a set of financial services deployed on public blockchain networks — primarily Ethereum and its layer-2 scaling solutions. Unlike conventional finance, which operates through centralized intermediaries, DeFi executes financial logic through immutable smart contracts.

items.name: Decentralized Exchanges (DEXs)
items.description: Peer-to-peer token trading via automated market makers. Liquidity providers deposit asset pairs into pools; traders swap against pool reserves.

items.name: Lending Protocols
items.description: Overcollateralized borrowing and lending markets. Suppliers deposit assets to earn variable-rate yield; borrowers post collateral to access liquidity without credit assessment.

items.name: Stablecoins
items.description: Tokens designed to maintain price parity with fiat currencies. Mechanisms include fiat-collateralized, crypto-overcollateralized, and algorithmic.

items.name: Derivatives & Synthetics
items.description: Tokenized financial contracts that derive value from underlying assets. Perpetual futures, options, and synthetic assets enable leveraged exposure and hedging.

items.name: Yield & Liquid Staking
items.description: Protocols that generate returns through staking, liquidity provision, and incentive distribution. Liquid staking derivatives tokenize staked positions.

items.name: Risk & Insurance
items.description: Decentralized coverage against smart-contract failure, oracle manipulation, and protocol insolvency. Members pool capital into mutuals.

items.name: Benefits & Risk Assessment
items.description: Any structural assessment of DeFi must weigh its transformative potential against the material risks that remain unresolved.

items.name: Establish Self-Custody
items.description: Deploy a non-custodial wallet. Record the seed phrase on physical media. This wallet becomes your identity layer and asset custodian.

items.name: Acquire Base-Layer Assets
items.description: Purchase ETH or SOL from a regulated on-ramp. Transfer a small test amount first. Gas tokens are required for network operations.

items.name: Execute a Calibrated Transaction
items.description: Connect your wallet to a major DEX or lending protocol. Execute a single, small transaction to observe gas mechanics and confirmation latency.

items.name: Scale Deliberately
items.description: Study protocol documentation, follow security researchers. Diversify across protocols and chains. Never allocate more than you are prepared to lose.
